arts.ttl: Nottingham Forest v Ipswich: FA Cup fifth round – live
arts.sum: Rolling report: Updates on the action at the City Ground FA Cup fifth round talking points | You can email Michael Will Unwin, our correspondent at the City Ground tonight, has been in touch to say he’s been reliably informed that England manager Thomas Tuchel is in attendance tonight (with his first game less than three weeks away). The bad news for Tuchel is that Morgan Gibbs-White and Liam Delap are both on their side’s respective benches. Could Alex Palmer wiggle his way into being Tuchel’s third-choice keeper? He’s certainly got an outside chance, given recent performances. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Nottingham Forest vs. Ipswich Town team news: Starting lineups confirmed for FA Cup fifth round tie
arts.sum: Nottingham Forest and Ipswich Town announce their starting lineups for Monday's FA Cup fifth-round fixture at the City Ground.

Nottingham Forest vs Ipswich: Preview, predictions and lineups
arts.sum: Nottingham Forest welcome Ipswich Town to the City Ground for the final tie of the FA Cup fifth round, with the winner to face Brighton & Hove Albion in the quarter-finals. Their only victories in their last ten matches came in this competition against lower league sides Bristol Rovers and Coventry City, but they will harbour slim hopes of making it beyond the high-flying Forest on Monday.

Marseille expect to lose Michael Murillo for several weeks
arts.sum: Michael Murillo (29) has played a prominent role under Roberto De Zerbi at Olympique de Marseille. He had started 12 of the last 13 games in Ligue 1 for OM…

arts.sum: Ipswich Town head to Nottingham Forest tonight aiming to reach the FA Cup quarter-finals. The final tie of the fifth round will see The Tractor Boys take on high-flying Forest at the City Ground. Brighton await the winners of tonight’s tie in the last eight. arts.ttl: Nottingham Forest vs Ipswich – Predicted lineup and team news
arts.sum: Nottingham Forest host Ipswich Town tonight in the final fixture of the FA Cup’s fifth round. Forest are flying high in the Premier League table and can secure progress to the FA Cup quarter-finals for just the second time in 29 years tonight.
